overview: From 2026-06-30 to 2026-07-01, elizaos/eliza had 60 new PRs (62 merged), 0 new issues, and 8 active contributors.

topPRs.title: release: promote develop → main (apps-launch domain/security fast-follows + current develop)
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
Production release: promotes the current `develop` to `main` (prod Cloudflare deploy — `api.elizacloud.ai`, `elizacloud.ai`, `app.elizacloud.ai`).

Includes the apps-launch fast-follows just merged to develop:
- #10297 — apps money/security: domain-buy orphan + cross-tenant takeover + charge-callback channel cross-tenant guard + refund/idempotency tests (#10253, #10247).
- #10303 — domain renewal-billing cron + custom-domain health probe (#10245, #10244).
- #10307 — domain search + buy UI in the Applications domains tab (#10246).
- #10312 — chat 404-recovery replay now streams (#10231).

…plus all other work currently on `develop`. Authorized as a full prod release.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

topPRs.title: test(plugin-browser): wire a MiniWoB++ benchmark through the real BROWSER command surface (#9476)
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
## What

Closes the one Definition-of-Done deliverable [verified missing on `develop`](https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/issues/9476#issuecomment-4830133507) for #9476:

> **≥1 benchmark wired through `plugin-browser`** (mirror `plugin-computeruse/src/osworld/adapter.ts`), with a committed run artifact.

The other three DoD items already landed (CI-asserted parity matrix, JSDOM real-code lane, typed error contract). The web benchmarks (Mind2Web, WebShop, VisualWebBench) all **bypass** plugin-browser via the inference layer — no benchmark drove the BROWSER command surface end-to-end. This adds one that does.

## How

A **MiniWoB++-style** web-interaction benchmark (the canonical web-agent benchmark) under `plugins/plugin-browser/src/benchmark/`, the plugin-browser analog of `plugin-computeruse/src/osworld/`:

- **`adapter.ts`** — `BrowserBenchmarkAdapter` with the same observation/action/step seam as `OSWorldAdapter`, driving an engine-agnostic `BrowserCommandExecutor`. The workspace executor binds the **real** `executeBrowserWorkspaceCommand` router (the same mock-free path the `browser-workspace-web-real-code` lane uses) — no mock service stands in for the browser.
- **`tasks.ts`** — 6 tasks (`click-button`, `click-link`, `enter-text`, `enter-text-dynamic`, `click-checkboxes`, `multistep-purchase`). Pure markup, **no page scripts** (web mode hard-blocks script exec, GHSA-mhhr-9ph9-64j7); deterministic per seed; reward computed from **observable DOM state** read back through real BROWSER `get` commands (`value`/`checked`/`url`/`title`).
- **`policy.ts`** — `OraclePolicy` (deterministic solver) + `NoopPolicy`/`WrongPolicy` negative baselines.
- **`runner.ts`** — `runBenchmarkSuite` drives every task×seed episode, scores it, aggregates.
- **`__tests__/miniwob-adapter.test.ts`** — CI-asserted real-code lane (runs in the default `vitest run`).
- **`scripts/run-miniwob-benchmark.mjs`** + `bench:miniwob` — runnable harness that writes a JSON run report.

## Evidence (real, on a Windows 11 host) — see `.github/issue-evidence/9476-browser-benchmark/`

| Policy | Result | Proves |
|--------|--------|--------|
| oracle | **18/18 solved (100%)** | the suite is solvable end-to-end through real BROWSER commands; every action step records `resultMode: "web"` (the live router ran it) |
| noop   | **0/18** | doing nothing scores 0 — reward is grounded in real DOM state, not hard-coded |
| wrong  | **0/18** | wrong-target / wrong-text near-misses score 0 — reward discriminates |

Test lane locally: **5/5 passing**. Benchmark sources type-check clean (verified with a resolvable `@types/node`; the package's default `typecheck` hits a pre-existing worktree `typeRoots` quirk unrelated to this change). The broader plugin-browser suite's 8 core-barrel-dependent suites can't load under this box's light-install (a pre-existing `uuid`→`drizzle-orm`→`yaml`→`@noble/*` cascade, identical before this change); all 80 runnable tests pass, including the new lane.

## Reproduce

```bash
bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-browser test -- src/benchmark   # CI lane
bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-browser bench:miniwob           # oracle (writes artifact)
bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-browser bench:miniwob -- --policy noop
bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-browser bench:miniwob -- --policy wrong
```

## Deferred (tracked, not dropped)

The remaining #9476 "Needs CI infra" items — a real-Chromium engine lane, an external-dataset benchmark (Mind2Web/WebArena) through plugin-browser, a web-element grounding benchmark, and CI gating — are tracked in follow-up #10333. The adapter is already engine-agnostic, so the real-Chromium lane is a new executor + gated lane, not a rewrite.

Closes #9476.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

topPRs.title: feat(first-run): onboarding entirely in the real floating chat — delete the onboarding gate/surface/voice stack (#9952)
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
Closes #9952. **Onboarding now happens inside the SAME `ContinuousChatOverlay` used everywhere** — the chat is the first surface, the separate onboarding UI is deleted. Net **+2,144 / −23,475** (≈ −21k LOC).

Built on the chat-centric design @lalalune/the fleet drafted in **#10268** (`IN_CHAT_ONBOARDING_DESIGN.md`) — that PR is **design-only and still unmerged** (its design doc is **not** on develop). This branch is the full implementation + the real-shell e2e it called for. (Coordinating: this is a separate branch; reconcile with #10268 as you prefer.)

## What
- **Land in chat.** `firstRunComplete=false` is now shell-paintable: a fresh profile paints the homescreen + auto-opened `ContinuousChatOverlay` — no full-screen `StartupFirstRunBackground`/`FirstRunChat` gate.
- **Seed onboarding into the live transcript.** A headless conductor (`use-first-run-conductor.ts`) pushes synthetic assistant messages: greeting → `[CHOICE:first-run id=runtime]` (Cloud/Local/Other) → Cloud OAuth via the message `secretRequest` field → `[CHOICE:first-run id=provider]` (role-correct default) → `[CHOICE:first-run id=tutorial]` (Take tutorial / Skip). Renders via the existing `InlineWidgetText` + `SensitiveRequestBlock` — **no new widget components**.
- **Display-only widgets; logic in a use case.** First-run choice values (`__first_run__:` sentinel) are intercepted in `sendActionMessage` and routed to `first-run-finish.ts` (the surviving `finishLocal`/`finishCloud`/`selectOrProvision`/`submitFirstRun` + `first-run-config` defaults). `POST /api/first-run` fires **exactly once** (module-guarded). Tutorial pick → existing `tutorial-controller.startTutorial`.
- **Deleted** (≈23k lines): `CompactOnboarding`, `use-first-run-controller` (1,454-LOC step machine) + 3 tests, `AgentPicker`, `onboarding-intent`, the dedicated first-run **voice stack** (`first-run-tts-route` + unwired from app-core `server.ts`, `voice-readiness`, `onboarding-voice-lines`, `synthesizeFirstRunSpeech`), the dead `.eliza-onboarding-overlay-shell` CSS, and the dead `CompactOnboarding` e2e harness.

## E2E — the real-shell ui-smoke lane (your #1 ask), all green
Rewrote the onboarding ui-smoke specs to drive the **real built shell** through the in-chat flow (no stubs):
- `onboarding-to-home.spec.ts` — **Local + Cloud + tutorial-start**, asserts the first surface is `continuous-chat-overlay` with the greeting, the old gate testids (`startup-first-run-background`/`first-run-chat`/`first-run-greeting`) are **count-0**, and `POST /api/first-run` fired **exactly once** on both paths. Desktop + **mobile**.
- Swept + green: `first-run-startup`, `runtime-configurability`, `computer-use`, `model-download-deferral`, `reset-returns-to-onboarding`, `assistant-home-flow`, `walkthrough-capture-smoke`, and **`full-walkthrough` 25/25 steps** (incl. onboarding). Per-step screenshots in `aesthetic-audit-output/onboarding-to-home/`.
- The implementation ran correctly in a real browser on the first attempt (chat-first paint, sentinel routing, cloud bind, deferred-completion-at-tutorial, exactly-once POST).

## Verification
`packages/ui` typecheck clean; 140 first-run/startup/shell unit tests pass; biome clean; **no dangling imports** of any deleted module (repo-wide grep).

## Pre-merge fixes applied (review-panel follow-ups)
- **Dead code excised.** The unreachable legacy step-machine finish chain in `useFirstRunCallbacks.ts` — `runFirstRunChatHandoff`, `handleFirstRunFinish`, `advanceFirstRun` / `handleFirstRunNext`, `handleCloudFirstRunFinish` (all confirmed zero live callers) — is **removed**, along with its now-orphaned imports/helpers, two dead exported helpers (`buildFirstRunStyleVoiceConfig`, `buildFirstRunCapabilitySubmitPayload`), and an orphaned `handleCloudLoginRef`. `AppContextValue` (`handleFirstRunNext`, `handleCloudFirstRunFinish`) and the AppContext plumbing were updated to match. `packages/ui` typecheck + biome clean.
- **Tutorial driven frame-by-frame in the walkthrough + CI-wired.** The tour cards now stamp `data-tutorial-step-id` (`TutorialSpotlight`/`TutorialOverlay`/tutorial-fixture); the full-walkthrough journey drives the real in-chat onboarding (greeting → runtime CHOICE → provider) and walks all 8 tutorial frames via that attribute. Added a keyless **`app-walkthrough-mock`** job to `scenario-pr.yml` on every PR (wired into the summary gate + artifact upload) and a **`walkthrough-live`** job to `app-live-e2e.yml` (real backend + model + vision verdicts + artifacts).

## Honest follow-up (tracked, not silently dropped)
- **Device remote-connect-at-URL onboarding (Android/iOS) — regressed, tracked in #10322.** The conductor offers Cloud/Local/Other; `runtime:other` runs the **local** backend and there is **no in-chat "connect to a remote host agent at a URL" step** (`finishRemote` exists in `first-run-finish.ts` but is unreachable — the in-chat choice channel only routes discrete button values, with no text/URL capture seam). Wiring it is a genuine **product/design decision** (a `runtime:other` URL sub-flow vs. a deep-link vs. a Settings entry), so rather than guess: the Android lane spec (`onboarding-to-home.android.spec.ts`) is `test.skip(true, …)` with the issue link, and the iOS smoke (`runIosOnboardingSmokeIfRequested` + `ios-onboarding-smoke.mjs`) now self-skips cleanly (the `NEEDS-HUMAN-REDESIGN` markers are removed). Both quarantines link **#10322**, which captures the redesign + how to un-skip.
- A final adversarial code-review is recommended given the deletion footprint.

topPRs.title: test(voice): real Apple Stage-B STT measurement + transcript WS hop + swabble wake gate (#9958)
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
Advances the live-voice e2e umbrella (#9958) with the remaining work that is
software/CI-tractable on a macOS (Apple Silicon) host, and precisely documents
the device-gated remainder. **Does not close #9958** — real on-device round-trip
recordings and the Android/iOS device lanes still need hardware.

## What this adds (all verified on-host)

### 1. Stage-B on-device STT evaluation — Apple `SFSpeechRecognizer` arm (real, measured)
`stt.stage-b.evaluation` was a hard skip stub (`--stage-b-eval-placeholder` → `exit(2)`).
`SFSpeechRecognizer` with `requiresOnDeviceRecognition=true` is reachable + authorized on
this Mac, so it's now a real measurement:
- `packages/scripts/stage-b-stt-bench/` — Swift package (file recognition → latency/RTF/hypothesis).
- `packages/scripts/stage-b-stt-bench.mjs` — synthesises **real speech** on-device
  (`say` → 16 kHz WAV) for the labelled corpus (the repo's checked-in `asr_bench` WAVs are
  deterministic tones, not speech), runs a quiet + 10 dB-noise condition, scores WER with the
  same word-edit metric as `native/verify/asr_bench.ts`, writes a matrix + markdown + audio.
- New matrix cell `stt.stage-b.apple-sfspeech` (`probe: stageBSttApple`, macOS); the old
  `stt.stage-b.evaluation` cell is narrowed to the genuinely device-gated iOS-battery +
  Android `SpeechRecognizer` (NNAPI) arms.

| Condition | Exact-accept | Mean WER | Mean latency | p50 | p90 | Mean RTF |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| quiet | 100% | 0.0% | 243 ms | 80 ms | 844 ms | 0.168 |
| noisy-10dB | 80% | 4.0% | 352 ms | 110 ms | 1267 ms | 0.245 |

RTF < 1 + exact accept on clean speech confirms the VOICE_UX.md §7 Apple Stage-B claim.
**Kokoro TTS unchanged.** Audio + JSON + MD: `.github/issue-evidence/9958-stt-stage-b-eval/`.

### 2. Transcripts server→WS→renderer round-trip test
`#10280`'s `transcript-realaudio.spec.ts` covered capture→record→view→playback→attach and
bridge parity, but its own scope note deferred the server `START`/`STOP_TRANSCRIPTION` →
`agent_event{stream:"voice-control"}` → renderer `dispatchVoiceControl` hop to #9958. That
renderer branch (`startup-phase-hydrate.ts`) had no test.
`startup-phase-hydrate.voice-control.test.ts` (4 tests, all pass) closes it.

### 3. iOS native-bridge tests run for real + extended
Both Swift contract suites execute and pass on-host (logs under `9958-native-bridge-swift-run/`):
TalkMode 3/3, Swabble now 9/9. Added `SwabbleTriggerGateTests.swift` covering the
production-used `SwabbleWakeBridgeContract.isTriggerOnly` trigger-only wake gate, previously
unexercised.

### 4. Matrix completeness
`bun run voice:matrix` full probe → `9958-voice-matrix-full-probe/`: every cell is `pending`
(host-runnable) or an explicit `skip` with a hardware reason — **no silent gaps**.

## Per-criterion status

| # | Acceptance criterion | Status |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | matrix doc + `voice:matrix`, no silent gaps | **done** (#10255; this PR adds the Apple Stage-B cell) |
| 2 | real mic→ASR→agent→Kokoro→speaker on macOS/iOS/Android + recordings | **device handoff** |
| 3 | live barge-in | web covered (#10305); real-device = handoff |
| 4 | talkmode/swabble Kotlin/Swift tests | **done** (#10255; Swift verified + extended here; Android = CI lane) |
| 5 | transcripts capture→…→attach e2e, action+button identical | **done** (#10280 + this PR's WS-hop test) |
| 6 | wakeword-context vs real openWakeWord head | **device/model handoff** (coordinate #9880/#10351) |
| 7 | iOS SFSpeechRecognizer vs Android vs fused STT matrix | **Apple arm measured here**; Android-NNAPI + iOS-battery + fused = handoff |
| 8 | `voice-live-e2e.yml` not solely Linux-X64 | **done** (#10255 added macOS/iOS/Android lanes) |

## Remaining device handoff
Full table in `.github/issue-evidence/9958-remaining-work-handoff.md`: real round-trip
recordings on macOS/Electrobun + iOS + Android, real-device barge-in, openWakeWord head,
iOS per-frame battery + Android `SpeechRecognizer` (NNAPI), and the Android native-bridge
Kotlin tests (need a full `bun install` + `cap:sync:android` so `@capacitor/android` resolves;
CI's `voice-android-matrix` lane does this).

## Reproduce
```bash
node packages/scripts/stage-b-stt-bench.mjs
node packages/scripts/voice-matrix.mjs --run --platform stt.stage-b.apple-sfspeech
swift test --disable-index-store --package-path plugins/plugin-native-swabble/ios
bun run --cwd packages/ui vitest run src/state/startup-phase-hydrate.voice-control.test.ts
node packages/scripts/voice-matrix.mjs
```

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

topPRs.title: fix(agent): finish #9948/#9949 security role gates
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
## Summary
- Finish the #9949 should-respond prompt-injection/social-engineering gate with a live scenario and manually reviewed evidence.
- Refresh #9948/#9949 role-boundary security evidence on current `develop`.
- Clean current-base type/lint ratchets touched by the final verify pass.

## Verification
- `bun install` after rebasing onto `origin/develop` (`8e09d5b709b`).
- `bun run verify` passed: 495 successful / 495 total, 5m32.495s, including post-audits (`audit-build-typecheck`, `audit-turbo-build-deps`, `audit-tee-secret-leak`, `audit-scripts`, and 28 dist-path config checks).
- Live model scenario: `security.should-respond-injection-gate`, run `50792b71-9327-4ba9-bbde-198f24e3a09f`, provider `openai`, result 1 passed / 0 failed / 0 skipped.
- Focused security/API checks listed in `.github/issue-evidence/9948-9949-security-role-gates-current.md`.

## Evidence
- `.github/issue-evidence/9949-should-respond-injection-gate.md`
- `.github/issue-evidence/9949-should-respond-injection-gate.report.json`
- `.github/issue-evidence/9949-should-respond-injection-gate.log`
- `.github/issue-evidence/9949-should-respond-injection-gate-viewer/`
- `.github/issue-evidence/9948-9949-security-role-gates-current.md`

## N/A
- App UI screenshots/audit: no `packages/app` UI change.
- Native/mobile/desktop capture: no native surface change.
- Audio/video walkthrough: no voice, TTS, STT, or media playback change.
- Chain/wallet artifacts: no blockchain or wallet transaction path change.

Closes #9948
Closes #9949
